== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[Heian period]] and [[medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[Mongol invasions of Japan]].
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Military rituals|ja|勝軍治要祓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military rituals|qq=}}

== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[Mongol invasions of Japan]].

In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.

== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Gunshin]][[ja:軍神]][[ko:군신]][[tr:Kyūgunshin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11637440]] Wikidata name: gunshin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunshin | native_name = 軍神 | native_name_lang= ja | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = God of war | known_for = Worshipped in Japanese history as a deity of military success and valor }}
'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A [[:en:God|God]] who governs [[:en:Military|Military]] affairs and [[:en:War|War]], often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A [[:en:Soldier|Soldier]] who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].
== References == <references />

== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.

=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''

=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].

=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Military rituals|ja|勝軍治要祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military rituals|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Gunshin]][[ja:軍神]][[ko:군신]][[tr:Kyūgunshin]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637440]] Wikidata name: gunshin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunshin | native_name = 軍神 | native_name_lang= ja | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = God of war | known_for = Worshipped in Japanese history as a deity of military success and valor }}
'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A [[:en:God|God]] who governs [[:en:Military|Military]] affairs and [[:en:War|War]], often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A [[:en:Soldier|Soldier]] who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Military rituals|ja|勝軍治要祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military rituals|qq=}}

== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.

'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A [[:en:God|God]] who governs [[:en:Military|Military]] affairs and [[:en:War|War]], often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A [[:en:Soldier|Soldier]] who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.

== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.

=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A [[:en:God|God]] who governs [[:en:Military|Military]] affairs and [[:en:War|War]], often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A [[:en:Soldier|Soldier]] who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].

In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingu|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]]''' ([[Katori Jingu|Katori Shrine]]) * '''[[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]]''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' * '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''[[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]''' * '''[[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]]'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''[[:en:Asakura Norikage|Asakura Sōteki]]''' of [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]], renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''[[:en:Tachibana Dōsetsu|Tachibana Dōsetsu]]''' of [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''[[:en:Uesugi Kenshin|Uesugi Kenshin]]''' of [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]], often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a [[:en:naval officer|naval officer]] known for his heroism during the [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]].
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of [[:en:Liaoyang|Liaoyang]].
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]].
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Military rituals|ja|勝軍治要祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military rituals|qq=}}

=== Sengoku Period === * '''{{ill|Asakura Norikage|en|Asakura Norikage|WD=Q859948|lt=Asakura Sōteki}}''' of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}, renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''{{ill|Tachibana Dōsetsu|en|Tachibana Dōsetsu|WD=Q6379490|lt=Tachibana Dōsetsu}}''' of {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''{{ill|Uesugi Kenshin|en|Uesugi Kenshin|WD=Q311080|lt=Uesugi Kenshin}}''' of {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}}, often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a {{ill|naval officer|en|naval officer|WD=Q10669499|lt=naval officer}} known for his heroism during the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}.
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of {{ill|Liaoyang|en|Liaoyang|WD=Q75203|lt=Liaoyang}}.
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.

{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunshin | native_name = 軍神 | native_name_lang= ja | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = God of war | known_for = Worshipped in Japanese history as a deity of military success and valor }}
'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A {{ill|God|en|God|WD=Q190|lt=God}} who governs {{ill|Military|en|Military|WD=Q8473|lt=Military}} affairs and {{ill|War|en|War|WD=Q198|lt=War}}, often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A {{ill|Soldier|en|Soldier|WD=Q4991371|lt=Soldier}} who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.

== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''{{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}''' * '''{{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}}'''

== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a {{ill|naval officer|en|naval officer|WD=Q10669499|lt=naval officer}} known for his heroism during the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}.

=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a {{ill|naval officer|en|naval officer|WD=Q10669499|lt=naval officer}} known for his heroism during the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}.
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of {{ill|Liaoyang|en|Liaoyang|WD=Q75203|lt=Liaoyang}}.
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.

{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunshin | native_name = 軍神 | native_name_lang= ja | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = God of war | known_for = Worshipped in Japanese history as a deity of military success and valor }}
'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A {{ill|God|en|God|WD=Q190|lt=God}} who governs {{ill|Military|en|Military|WD=Q8473|lt=Military}} affairs and {{ill|War|en|War|WD=Q198|lt=War}}, often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A {{ill|Soldier|en|Soldier|WD=Q4991371|lt=Soldier}} who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}}''' ([[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi}}''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''{{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}''' * '''{{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}}'''

== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:

{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunshin | native_name = 軍神 | native_name_lang= ja | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = God of war | known_for = Worshipped in Japanese history as a deity of military success and valor }}
'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A {{ill|God|en|God|WD=Q190|lt=God|1=God}} who governs {{ill|Military|en|Military|WD=Q8473|lt=Military|1=Military}} affairs and {{ill|War|en|War|WD=Q198|lt=War|1=War}}, often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A {{ill|Soldier|en|Soldier|WD=Q4991371|lt=Soldier|1=Soldier}} who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}''' ([[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''{{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}''' * '''{{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}}'''

== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}''' ([[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''{{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|qid=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}''' * '''{{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}}'''
=== Sengoku Period === * '''{{ill|Asakura Norikage|en|Asakura Norikage|qid=Q859948|lt=Asakura Sōteki|1=Asakura Norikage}}''' of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}, renowned for his strategic prowess. * '''{{ill|Tachibana Dōsetsu|en|Tachibana Dōsetsu|qid=Q6379490|lt=Tachibana Dōsetsu|1=Tachibana Dōsetsu}}''' of {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, famous as Kyushu's god of war. * '''{{ill|Uesugi Kenshin|en|Uesugi Kenshin|qid=Q311080|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|1=Uesugi Kenshin}}''' of {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|qid=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}, often called the "war god" due to his devotion to [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]] and unmatched military skills.
== Modern Military Deities in Japan == From the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} onward, soldiers who died bravely in combat, such as [[Nogi Maresuke]] and [[Tōgō Heihachirō]], were revered as military deities. Examples include:
=== Tamejirō Hirose === {{main|Hirose Tamejirō}} [[Hirose Tamejirō]], a {{ill|naval officer|en|naval officer|qid=Q10669499|lt=naval officer|1=Naval officer}} known for his heroism during the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}.
=== Sōta Tachibana === {{main|Tachibana Sōta}} Renowned for his actions at the Battle of {{ill|Liaoyang|en|Liaoyang|qid=Q75203|lt=Liaoyang|1=Liaoyang}}.
=== Taketsuna Sasaki === {{main|Sasaki Taketsuna}} Famous for his role in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|qid=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}.
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Military rituals|ja|勝軍治要祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military rituals|qq=}}

{{Citations needed|date=August 27, 2020}} {{Infobox person | name = Kunshin | native_name = 軍神 | native_name_lang= ja | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | nationality = Japanese | occupation = God of war | known_for = Worshipped in Japanese history as a deity of military success and valor }}
'''Kunshin''' (軍神) refers to:
# A {{ill|God|en|God|qid=Q190|lt=God|1=God}} who governs {{ill|Military|en|Military|qid=Q8473|lt=Military|1=Military}} affairs and {{ill|War|en|War|qid=Q198|lt=War|1=War}}, often referred to as '''Bujin''' (武神) or '''Toshin''' (闘神). # A {{ill|Soldier|en|Soldier|qid=Q4991371|lt=Soldier|1=Soldier}} who was [[:en:Deification|deified]] after a heroic [[death in battle]].
== Overview ==
'''Kunshin''' refers to a deity believed to bring victory in battle and to protect warriors. In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, gods such as [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Futsunushi]], who played roles in subduing [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]], were worshipped at [[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]] and [[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]], respectively, as military gods. Additionally, the [[Great Deity of Suwa]] (諏訪大社の祭神), [[Takeminakata]], is also considered a god of war.
== Traditional War Deities == In Japan, the following deities are worshipped as war gods: * '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}''' ([[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}}''' ([[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]]) * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}''' ([[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]]) – Revered as "Japan's foremost war god." * '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}''' ([[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]]) * '''{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}'''
== Japanese Middle and Early Modern Eras == During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} and [[:en:Medieval Japan|Medieval Japan]], the [[:en:Genji clan|Genji clan]] (源氏) worshipped the [[Hachiman deity]] (八幡神) as a clan deity, spreading its influence as a military god.
=== Resurrected War Gods === * '''{{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|qid=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}''' * '''{{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}}'''
